description: The first REAL knowledge graph on the KnowledgeAsset mechanism (MagDynamics Requirements 4-5): corporate knowledge (ECARMF docs, architecture, coding standards, AI prompts, SOPs, training) plus culture documents (mission/values, leadership principles, employee handbook) as one graph with typed relationship edges and semanticSearchEnabled flags. Day-one scope is PHASED per open question 4: full asset storage + relationships now; the semantic-search execution engine is a later capability the flags already opt into. Touches the ECARMF-006 Knowledge Graph Standard (still packaged in the repo - noted, not unpacked).
name: MagDynamics Knowledge Graph

knowledgeAssets.title: MagDynamics Mission, Vision & Core Values
knowledgeAssets.summary: Why we exist, where we are going, and the values every decision is tested against.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Mission: build enterprise AI systems that make organizations measurably smarter. Core values: (1) Evidence over opinion. (2) Ship, then sharpen. (3) The client's data is sacred. (4) Teach everything twice. (5) Own the outcome.

knowledgeAssets.title: Leadership Principles & Operational Philosophy
knowledgeAssets.summary: How leaders at MagDynamics decide, delegate, and give feedback - the operating expression of the core values.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Leaders write decisions down with their reasoning. Escalate with options, not problems. Feedback is specific, kind, and tied to a named value. Quality bars are non-negotiable; scope is.

knowledgeAssets.title: Employee Handbook - 2026 edition
knowledgeAssets.summary: Employment policies: remote work, PTO, conduct, equipment, confidentiality.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Remote-first with quarterly on-sites. Unlimited PTO with a 15-day minimum. Client confidentiality survives employment. Company equipment is inventoried as ITAssets and returned on exit.

knowledgeAssets.title: Compensation Policy - KPI-linked component
knowledgeAssets.summary: How the variable compensation component reads employee-level KPIs (utilization, training) - the policy behind Requirement 8.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Variable comp reads the employee's KPI actuals (subjectType User): billable utilization vs target and training hours vs target, averaged over the review period. KPIs inform the number; a human leadership review decides it. No automated compensation changes, ever.

knowledgeAssets.title: ECARMF Kernel Architecture Overview
knowledgeAssets.summary: The kernel executes declarations; packages carry domain knowledge; tenants are isolated registries.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Nine tenant-scoped registries. Packages declare entities/rules/KPIs/agents/knowledge assets and are versioned, never edited in place. The kernel contains zero domain knowledge. Every financial event is audited; AI proposes, humans approve.

knowledgeAssets.title: Development & Coding Standards
knowledgeAssets.summary: House rules for shipping code: tests with every change, migrations never edited after release, packages over kernel changes.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Every behavior change ships with a test. EF migrations are append-only once released. Prefer a package update to a kernel change; prefer configuration to a package update. API endpoints enforce permissions before touching stores.

knowledgeAssets.title: AI Prompt Library & Agent Persona Standards
knowledgeAssets.summary: How agent personas are written: grounded, advisory-only, disclaimers where liability exists.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Personas ground every statement in provided context, never invent data, and recommend professionals for legal/tax decisions. Liability-bearing agents declare an outputDisclaimer, which the kernel enforces at the output layer.

knowledgeAssets.title: Client Onboarding Methodology
knowledgeAssets.summary: The tenant test-case playbook: requirements doc, provisional decisions marked TBD, mechanisms before data, live verification before commit.
knowledgeAssets.contentText: Every client build starts from a requirements test-case document. Unknowns become clearly-labeled provisional placeholders, never blockers. Kernel mechanisms are proven with live API verification before anything is committed. Reuse is checked before anything new is built.
